The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can literally count them,” said the aid volunteer, whose name has been changed for fear of retribution.

events.summary:
The programme, aimed at keeping 1m girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, withdrawn after aid cuts

A leading higher education programme, aimed at keeping 1 million girls in school across Africa, Asia and the Middle East, has been axed by the British government just two years after it was announced.

The scheme, Strengthening higher education for female empowerment (SHEFE), which was unveiled with some fanfare two years ago by the outgoing Conservative government, had a £45m budget to increase access to higher education for 1 million students worldwide. It has now had its tender withdrawn, the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) said.

events.title: ‘I can only describe it as a war zone’: the rescuers navigating Venezuela’s post-quake hellscape
events.summary:
Thousands of volunteers are joined by overseas teams in the hope of finding more survivors in the rubble, reports Tom Phillips in Caraballeda.

Photography and video by Manu Quintero

When twin earthquakes tore through Venezuela’s northern coast last week, Israel Rivas was at home hundreds of miles away in the industrial city of San Félix. As the scale of the catastrophe became clear, the 24-year-old knew he had to react. A mechanic and budding photographer, Rivas gathered the money he had been saving to buy a new camera lens and jumped on a bus to make the 12-hour journey to La Guaira, the coastal state that has suffered the most damage.

“I couldn’t eat well. I couldn’t sleep well, knowing that my brothers and sisters from this country are dying, so I … came here and I’m doing the best I can,” he said on Wednesday, exactly a week after the disaster, as he stood outside Residencia La Gabarra, a 12-storey block of beachside apartments that had collapsed into a jumble of reinforced concrete and bricks with at least three children inside.

Continue reading...

A wave of Russian missiles and drones has struck across Ukraine, killing 21 people, and heavily damaging apartment blocks and other buildings, in an attack on the eve of a Nato summit in Turkey that has exposed widening gaps in Ukraine’s air defences.

Fifteen people were killed in Kyiv, Russia’s main target, and 56 others were injured, according to the city’s administrative head, Tymur Tkachenko. Another six people were killed in the wider Kyiv region and 21 were injured, according to Mykola Kalashnyk, the head of the regional administration, and other emergency officials.

Authorities in Turkey have widened a crackdown on public life, arresting more than 200 people during raids across Ankara last month, jailing a comedian and blocking a cruise ship carrying LGBTQ+ passengers from docking in the run-up to the Nato summit in the capital.

The arrests followed a ban on demonstrations in Ankara that was put in place until 10 July. Human Rights Watch (HRW) said this was evidence of Turkey’s “ruthless intolerance of freedom of speech and assembly”. The watchdog group said the Nato summit, which starts on Tuesday, was taking place in the context of intensifying violations of basic rights, “including far-reaching restrictions on the main political opposition party, the media, and freedom of expression in general”.

events.title: Detained Gaza doctor almost unrecognisable after injuries in Israeli jail, lawyer says
events.summary:
Hussam Abu Safiya faces ‘tangible danger to his life’ following 18 months in prison without charge or trial

  • Middle East crisis live – latest updates

One of Gaza’s most prominent doctors is almost unrecognisable because of severe injuries inflicted in Israeli detention, his lawyer has said, and faces “tangible danger to his life” after being held for 18 months without charge or trial.

Hussam Abu Safiya met his lawyer on 2 July, after a transfer to Israel’s notorious underground Rakefet prison in late June. He had difficulty breathing and speaking continuously, was so weak he struggled to sit upright, and repeatedly seemed on the verge of losing consciousness, said his lawyer, Nasser Odeh.

Continue reading...

events.title: Israeli command system identified 850,000 targets in Gaza and Lebanon wars, says supplier
events.summary:
Elbit Systems supplied Tzayad digital army programme to map people, vehicles and other objects in real time

Israel identified about 1,000 potential targets a day during the first two years of the wars in Gaza and Lebanon with its command and control system, according to a presentation by the country’s largest arms supplier, Elbit Systems.

A total of 850,000 targets were detected in real time by the Israeli Tzayad digital army programme across all the military’s theatres of war between 7 October and the end of 2025, the company said at a military conference in London.

Continue reading...

events.title: Millions join funeral procession for Iran’s supreme leader Ali Khamenei
events.summary:
Crowds swelled through Tehran as mourners dressed in black carried flags proclaiming: ‘We will rise’

A crowd of millions assembled on Monday for the funeral procession of Iran’s assassinated supreme leader, Ali Khamenei.

The scale and depth of the march, however engineered, represents an extraordinary turnaround for a country that only seven months ago was gripped by street protests at which thousands of people were killed by government security forces. Many will say the assembly was a monument to a misconceived war launched on Iran by Donald Trump in February.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistan roof collapse kills 14 children at tutoring centre
events.summary:
Local officials said preliminary reports showed the centre was unregistered and operating inside a privately owned residential building

Fourteen children died after ⁠the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed in Pakistan’s eastern city of ⁠Lahore on Tuesday, ⁠rescue ​officials have said, as authorities opened the way for a possible negligence ⁠investigation.

Punjab’s emergency service said rescuers found children and a 30-year-old female teacher ⁠under the rubble of the private after-school facility. The ​children killed were aged ‌five to ‌16 with most below nine.

Continue reading...

events.title: Delhi plans to ban petrol rickshaws and scooters in effort to cut toxic fumes
events.summary:
Government hopes for 30% of city’s fleet to be electric by 2030, in move hailed as ‘gamechanger’ on air pollution

The unruly chaos of Delhi’s roads would be unrecognisable without the rickshaws and scooters that zip through India’s capital in their millions, emitting toxic fumes in their wake. But now, ambitious policies aim to give the city’s most recognisable vehicles an environmental makeover.

On Monday, Delhi’s government announced plans to eventually ban petrol scooters, motorbikes and autorickshaws in favour of those running on electricity, in an attempt to bring down dangerously high pollution levels in the city by the end of the decade.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Humanity is a privilege’: Umar Khalid on his six years in an Indian jail without trial
events.summary:
Exclusive: Activist tells of his life as one of India’s most prominent political prisoners and his opposition to the government of Narendra Modi

Prison is hardest at sunset. As the thousands of prisoners incarcerated in Delhi’s most infamous jail are cast out of their cells and forced into the dank yard until darkness falls, prisoner number 626714 feels the punishing dread begin to rise.

Yet the inmate – better known as Umar Khalid – was recently moved to discover that another political prisoner, exiled at a camp thousands of miles from India, wrote of the very same feeling more than 150 years ago.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill dozens in eastern Afghanistan
events.summary:
Pakistan says strikes were aimed at a terrorist group while Taliban condemn ‘cowardly act of aggression’

Pakistani airstrikes in three eastern provinces of Afghanistan killed 36 civilians and wounded 163 others, Afghan officials have said, as attacks between the two countries showed no sign of abating.

Pakistan’s information minister, Attaullah Tarar, said the operations on Sunday night were aimed at a terrorist group his country blamed for a deadly militant attack in Karachi that killed three security personnel over the weekend.

Continue reading...

events.title: Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached ‘alarming’ levels since aid cuts, survey finds
events.summary:
Fears hard-won gains in reducing child mortality over 20 years are at risk after end of USAID funding for nutrition programmes

Child malnutrition in Nepal has reached “alarming” levels, according to the largest ever survey of under-fives in the country.

The new figures came just over a year after USAID, the former US flagship agency closed by the Trump administration in 2025, stopped funding work on child nutrition in Nepal.

Continue reading...

events.title: Celtic leaders doubt Burnham’s devolution drive will go beyond England’s borders
events.summary:
Would-be prime minister has made basic missteps in pitches to Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland

Andy Burnham’s devolution promises are yet to impress sceptical Celtic administrations hoping for a reset with Westminster, sources in Cardiff and Edinburgh have said.

Burnham, who is expected to take over from Keir Starmer as prime minister on 20 July, has made much of his support for the devolution of power and resources in England, pledging in an agenda-setting speech last week to make a new “No 10 North” the “nerve centre of a rewired Britain”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Farage warned attack against ‘establishment’ over unregistered gifts could lead to harsher punishment – UK politics live
events.summary:
Reform UK leader claims he is victim of ‘hit job’ as parliamentary standards commissioner investigates £5m donation

On the subject of Andy Burnham, the Financial Times is running a story today saying the access talks his team is holding with senior civil servants, intended to help Whitehall departments get ready to implement the new PM’s policy agenda, are being hindered by the fact that Burnham has not decided who will do the top cabinet jobs.

In the story, Lucy Fisher, George Parker and Anna Gross say:

Talks have not yet formally started with the Treasury and Burnham’s refusal to nominate a chancellor has complicated transition planning.

One Labour figure complained Burnham’s operation was “skeletal”, adding: “Access talks require a shadow cabinet. Burnham needs to nominate key people in advance or he cannot have meaningful talks” ….

Yes. And look – I’m not going to go into what we discussed privately, but everything I’ve seen from Andy publicly suggests that he knows that welfare reform is absolutely necessary … [because] it’s fundamentally about the life chances of a whole generation of young people.

And if we think the best option and best opportunity that we can gift as a country to a generation of young people is a life on benefits – are we serious?

My sense is that the appetite, both within the parliamentary Labour party and the new administration, will be absolutely up for doing this.

One of those is how we support young people. I will not defend an education system that is overly focused on the university route and does not lay out paths to technical qualifications for our young people. Too many young people get to year 10 at school, and they can’t see where school is taking them, because the system isn’t focusing on those young people.

And then, at 16, I believe we need the guarantee of a work placement for 16 to 18-year-olds, apprenticeships for every 16 to 18-year-old who wants one, and what I’ve done in Great Manchester is something that might be looked at more broadly, free bus travel for 16 to 18-year-olds, so that they can access those opportunities.

Continue reading...

events.title: GB News co-owner ‘cashing in on climate chaos’ after leap in fossil fuel investments, critics say
events.summary:
Exclusive: Campaigners argue news channel’s attacks on climate action ‘work in financial interests’ of Sir Paul Marshall

The hedge fund run by the co-owner of GB News almost tripled its investments in fossil fuel companies in the first quarter of 2026 to $2.8bn (£2.1bn), the Guardian can reveal.

Critics have accused Sir Paul Marshall of “cashing in on climate chaos” and have claimed the news channel, which frequently attacks climate science and action, was “working in its owner’s financial interests”.

Continue reading...

events.title: Wegovy weight-loss pill goes on sale on UK high street and online pharmacies
events.summary:
Thousands of people begin receiving their first deliveries of once-a-day medication made by Novo Nordisk

  • Business live – latest updates

A once-a-day Wegovy weight-loss pill has gone on sale at high street and online pharmacies in the UK, but is not yet available on the NHS.

Thousands of people began receiving their first deliveries of the pill, made by the Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk, on Monday. It contains the same active GLP-1 ingredient as the Wegovy jab, semaglutide, and is similarly effective, according to studies.

Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Smart operator’: how BT’s first female CEO helped turn company around
events.summary:
The firm’s share price has risen 80% under Allison Kirkby’s leadership – but pressure remains for her to deliver further growth

  • Business live – latest updates

If timing is everything, then Allison Kirkby may have judged it perfectly.

Since becoming BT’s first female chief executive more than two years ago the company’s share price has climbed 80%, an investor-pleasing turnaround that has seen Kirkby well-rewarded with a pay and bonus package of £5.6m last year, the largest for a boss of the telecoms company in well over a decade. However, there are questions over how much credit Kirkby can take for the apparent revival of the business.

Continue reading...

events.title: Republican senator Ted Cruz thanks Trump for ‘getting rid of that ridiculous red card’ in apparent reference to Folarin Balogun – live
events.summary:
President confirms he called Fifa to lobby against US striker’s suspension; senator weighs into furor

  • Trump confirms he asked Infantino for review of Folarin Balogun red card

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

Cruz just thanked Donald Trump for “getting rid of that ridiculous red card” – apparently in reference to Folarin Balogun. The US striker received a straight red card from referee Raphael Claus after a video review, but Fifa have now lifted Bolagun’s one-match ban, making him now available to play for the USA in their match against Belgium on Monday.

As my colleagues covering the World Cup note, despite the move being considered excessively harsh, Fifa’s disciplinary code does not permit appeals against straight red cards, with officials from the world governing body and US Soccer confirming after the game that the sanction could not be challenged.

Continue reading...

events.title: Seaplane lands in New York City’s East River with only minor injuries reported
events.summary:
Seaplane carrying eight people made ‘hard landing’ and was towed back to dock, according to the fire department

A seaplane made a rough landing in New York City’s East River on Sunday, alarming bystanders and resulting in two minor injuries, according to city authorities.

The seaplane carrying eight people made a “hard landing” at about noon, according to the New York City fire department. The plane made it upright and was towed back to dock, the department said.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump denied latest bid to delay $5.8m judgment payment to E Jean Carroll
events.summary:
Carroll was awarded damages after New York jury concluded Trump sexually abused her, then defamed her after she publicly described the attack

Donald Trump’s latest attempt to delay payment of a $5.8m judgment for defaming a magazine columnist whom a jury determined he sexually abused has been emphatically rejected by a federal court judge.

In a single-sentence 4 July order, US district Judge Lewis Kaplan denied the president’s request for more time to pay the civil judgment owed to E Jean Carroll, who was awarded the damages after a New York jury concluded that Trump sexually abused her in 1996 – then defamed her after she publicly described the attack in 2019.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump rings New York Stock Exchange bell to mark first trading day for ‘Trump accounts’
events.summary:
US president rings bell from White House and showcases initiative that gives children a $1,000 investment account

Donald Trump rang the bell of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) from the White House to mark the first trading day for Trump accounts, a government initiative that provides children with a $1,000 investment account.

The US president hosted leaders from the NYSE and the Nasdaq stock exchange at the Oval Office for a press conference on Monday morning. It is the first joint opening of the exchanges, and the first time the bell had been rung at the White House.

Continue reading...

events.title: Tennessee national guard troops shoot man dead in Memphis
events.summary:
Authorities claim Tyrin Johnson, 20, pulled gun on officers controversially deployed at instigation of Trump

National guard soldiers on patrol in Memphis shot and killed a man that authorities in the Tennessee city said turned and pulled a gun on the troops during a chase.

The shooting took place at about 4am on Sunday as the soldiers responded to a report of gunfire.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump, Mamdani give contrasting July 4th speeches. And, U.S. faces Belgium in World Cup
events.summary: For the nation's 250th birthday, President Trump and NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani had opposing visions of America. And, the U.S. and Belgium face off today in the FIFA World Cup.

events.title: Russian missile and drone attack on Ukraine's capital kills at least 12
events.summary: Russia launched waves of missiles and drones at Kyiv early Monday, killing at least 12 people in an attack that exposed widening gaps in Ukraine's air defenses, authorities said.

events.title: Trump won spending promises from NATO last year. This week, he'll try to enforce them
events.summary: President Donald Trump is heading to Ankara, Turkey, for the annual NATO summit. Last year, he pushed NATO allies to spend more on defense. This year, his mission is to try to enforce those pledges.

events.title: Y si sí: the phrase uniting Mexico during the World Cup
events.summary: Mexico's World Cup run has inspired a phrase heard across the country: "Y si sí." Or in English: "What if we can?" Anamaria Sayre reports from Mexico City.

events.title: NATO leaders look for unity as Trump attends annual summit
events.summary: As NATO leaders gather for this week's summit in Turkey, Europe is preparing to shoulder more of its own defense. David M. Cattler of the Center for European Policy Analysis explains why.

events.title: Hong Kong's 'sound of silence': 6 years under the national security law
events.summary: Six years after Beijing imposed its National Security Law, many of Hong Kong's opposition voices have gone silent. Danny Vincent looks at what's changed.

events.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
events.summary: In an unusual move, FIFA's Disciplinary Committee announced that it would delay Folarin Balogun's one-game red card ban. It came after President Trump spoke to FIFA President Gianni Infantino.

events.title: Super Typhoon Bavi brings destruction to Guam and surrounding Pacific islands
events.summary: People in the Northern Mariana Islands — remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean — are dealing with the aftermath of intense winds and severe flooding from Super Typhoon Bavi.

events.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
events.summary: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

events.title: A new phase in the war in Ukraine
events.summary: Ukraine says it can now hit military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Former ambassador Daniel Fried explains why he thinks Russia is starting to lose its strategic advantage.

events.title: Can India, Europe build a clean energy supply chain beyond China?
events.summary: With China dominating every step of the solar energy production chain, governments in India and Europe are looking to reduce their overreliance on a single supplier.

events.title: Weapons of long-ago conflicts still haunt the people of Laos
events.summary: As decommissioning teams seek out unexploded ordnance in paddy fields, forests and rural pastures, Laos prepares to host a review conference of the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

events.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
events.summary: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

events.title: Is Africa ready for El Nino?
events.summary: Experts warn the El Nino climate phenomenon could bring drought, floods and displacement to parts of Africa later this year. Are governments prepared?

events.title: Firefighters battle wildfires across southern Europe
events.summary: Wildfires are raging across several parts of southern Europe. They come on the heels of massive heat waves in much of the region.

events.title: Philippine Senate launches impeachment trial of Sara Duterte
events.summary: The trial against Sara Duterte is the latest escalation in a bitter feud between the Philippines' leading political dynasties. The proceedings could have major implications for the country's 2028 presidential elections.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
events.summary: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

events.title: Sri Lanka prison clashes leave dozens dead
events.summary: More than two dozen people have died and over 100 injured following a violent dispute between different prison populations in Negombo.

events.title: Hamas to dissolve Gaza governing body
events.summary: Officials for the militant organization have confirmed the move, which was agreed to earlier this year in a peace deal with Israel.

events.title: Iran: Millions flock to Ali Khamenei's funeral procession
events.summary: Crowds have thronged the streets of Tehran in a procession for Iran's late supreme leader. The huge event is seen as a show of strength by the theocratic regime amid peace negotiations with the US.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific
events.summary: Beijing carried out the launch in what is known as a nuclear-free zone due to a longstanding treaty against testing nuclear missiles in the area.

events.title: Why heat waves heighten the risk of blackouts
events.summary: When extreme heat hits, people turn to AC, but what if the grid fails? From France to the US, heat waves are pushing power systems to the limit, raising blackout risks and energy prices when electricity is needed most.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: India news: Mumbai building collapse kills six, as heavy rains batter city
events.summary: Heavy monsoon rains triggered building collapses, landslides and widespread transport disruption in India's financial capital, with forecasters warning of more rain. DW has the latest.

events.title: Germany news: Federal Cabinet approves draft 2027 budget
events.summary: The budget draft provides for more borrowing than initially planned, as well as a big increase in defense spending. Meanwhile, three men are facing trial over a massive stash of illegal weapons. Follow DW for more.

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Australia, Fiji sign defense pact to counter China in Pacific
events.summary: Fiji, which had grown closer to Beijing under its former leadership, will now consult Canberra on security developments.

events.title: Ukraine: Several killed in renewed attacks on Kyiv
events.summary: At least 12 people were killed when Russian ballistic missiles hit the Ukrainian capital. This follows the large-scale attack on Kyiv last Thursday.

events.title: Super Typhoon Bavi hits US Pacific islands
events.summary: Super Typhoon Bavi made landfall near Guam, a US territory located in the western Pacific Ocean. The storm brought powerful winds and heavy rain to the Northern Mariana Islands.

events.title: Far-right AfD reaffirms ambition to govern Germany
events.summary: The AfD has strengthened its hardline leadership despite ongoing scrutiny from Germany's domestic intelligence agencies. Co-leader Alice Weidel says the far-right party is ready to govern after strong polling gains.

events.title: Wildfires rage in Portugal, Greece, France and Spain
events.summary: Six countries in southern Europe have been hit by wildfires. One of the blazes, in southwestern France, could affect Monday's third stage of the Tour de France, authorities warn.

events.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
events.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

events.title: Albania PM Rama: Protests a stage for anti-Trump forces
events.summary: Protesters in Albania say their government is selling the country its nature out to foreign investors and luxury developers. Albanian Prime Minister Edi Rama tells DW why he thinks people are so upset.

events.title: Jehovah's Witnesses fight German state for Holocaust archive
events.summary: The Jehovah's Witnesses have secured a partial victory in a dispute over a unique archive documenting the community's persecution by the Nazi regime. It comes after a new memorial to the victims was unveiled in Berlin.

events.title: Ukraine strikes oil terminals near St. Petersburg in Russia
events.summary: The long-range strikes are the latest salvo in Ukraine’s expanding campaign to inflict economic damage on Russia and hinder its war machine.

events.title: Welcoming the Taliban? — Germany's new Afghanistan policy
events.summary: To facilitate the deportation of criminals to Afghanistan, the German government is allowing Taliban diplomats into the country.

events.title: Kielce pogrom in Poland: 80th anniversary of 1946 massacre
events.summary: Just over a year after World War II ended, a mob in the Polish city of Kielce killed around 40 Jews, some of them Holocaust survivors. A lie about ritual child murder escalated the violence.

events.title: Maltese tycoon faces trial over Caruana Galizia murder
events.summary: The Maltese businessman accused of masterminding journalist Daphne Caruana Galizia’s 2017 murder is in court — seven years after his arrest. Five other men have already been found guilty for their roles in the murder.

events.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
events.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

events.title: Jürgen Klopp and Germany: A partnership long in the making
events.summary: In one of the worst-kept secret in football, the former Dortmund and Liverpool coach looks to be on the verge of becoming Germany's new boss.

events.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
events.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

events.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
events.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

events.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
events.summary: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
events.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

events.title: AI drones made in Germany see duty on Ukraine's front line
events.summary: The German defense company Helsing is supplying combat drones to Ukraine and will soon provide them to the German army. How effective are they on the front line? DW joined a Ukrainian combat mission to find out.

events.title: German coalition agrees on changes to pensions, tax rates
events.summary: After its first year in office, Germany's coalition government plans to push through sweeping changes with the stated goal of reviving a sluggish economy. The plans met with a mixed reaction.

events.title: German public university creates Islamic theology faculty
events.summary: The University of Münster is the first public university in Europe to establish an Islamic theology faculty, a move that is is attracting international attention.

events.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
events.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

events.title: Turkish comedian Deniz Goktas detained at Istanbul airport
events.summary: Turkish stand-up comedian Deniz Goktas was detained on his return to Turkey, days after a video he posted online gained traction. The clip included references to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan and religion.

events.title: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers
events.summary: The Vatican's watchdog authority has imposed severe disciplinary measures on an ultratraditionalist Catholic group. The move comes after the group, the Society of Saint Pius X, consecrated bishops without papal consent.

events.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
events.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

events.title: Does football need FIFA and its World Cup?
events.summary: Though the popularity of the World Cup continues to grow, its organizing body, FIFA, has arguably never been less popular. So why does football still need FIFA? And is there any way out?

events.title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
events.summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. Many patients and staff were evacuated from the facility.

events.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
events.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

events.title: Explosion in Monaco: Who is Vadym Yermolaiev?
events.summary: Ukrainian-born businessman Vadym Yermolaiev was reportedly injured in a suspected assassination attempt in Monaco. Kyiv had previously imposed sanctions on him. What is known about the case?

events.title: Chancellor's message to Germany's World Cup exit backfires
events.summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

events.title: Russia's fuel crisis: Is Putin under pressure?
events.summary: Ukraine's extended campaign against Russian energy infrastructure is disrupting fuel supplies and military logistics. With shortages mounting in occupied Crimea, is the pressure pushing Putin toward negotiations?

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Hydration breaks: The most controversial issue at the World Cup
events.summary: The mandatory hydration breaks at the 2026 World Cup have sparked debate in the stands, on the pitch, and far beyond.

events.title: German prosecutors arrest Rwanda genocide suspect
events.summary: A German-Rwandan national has been arrested on suspicion of complicity in murders during Rwanda's 1994 genocide. He is thought to have been personally involved in the killing of one victim.

events.title: Ahead of NATO summit, Turkey arrests more than 200 activists
events.summary: Protesters are clearly unwelcome ahead of the upcoming NATO summit in Ankara. Amid a strict public gatherings ban, 225 human rights and environmental activists as well as journalists have already been arrested.

events.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
events.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

events.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
events.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

events.title: EU slaps €3 duty on low-value imported packages
events.summary: The EU has moved to curb what it sees as unfair competition from mostly Chinese online retailers by imposing its levy. It follows a similar move from the US.

events.title: Global firms and German startups compete in realizing nuclear fusion
events.summary: Companies around the world are competing to see who can build the first commercially viable nuclear fusion reactor. German startups are also in the race, supported by major corporations and private investors.

events.title: Germany: Heat wave is over, political fallout has just begun
events.summary: After a major heat wave, critics say leaders need to do more for climate change adaption. Policymakers disagree on who's responsible to prepare Germany for extreme temperatures ― the federal government or municipalities.

events.title: Angela Merkel unveils official portrait in Berlin
events.summary: On Tuesday former German Chancellor Merkel presented the painting for the official portrait gallery of the country's heads of government. She had previously inspired many artists.

events.title: Will alleged crime ties damage Serbia's ruling party?
events.summary: Pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic is growing as years of investigations into alleged links between the state and organized crime coincide with a gang war. Experts say the issue is eroding support within his base.

events.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
events.summary: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

events.title: Germany out of 2026 World Cup: What went wrong?
events.summary: Germany are out of the World Cup earlier than expected for the third straight time. How did this happen?

events.title: Germany out of World Cup after loss on penalties to Paraguay
events.summary: Germany lost in dramatic fashion against Paraguay in Boston to exit the 2026 World Cup at the first knockout stage.

events.title: Alarm over alleged abuse in French schools and nurseries
events.summary: Hundreds of alleged cases of physical and sexual assault against children in French nurseries and elementary schools have revealed flaws in the system, experts say. They argue that reforms are urgently needed.

events.title: Pride Month has great significance for those who feel invisible. In Slovenia, many LGBTQ+ people understand just what that means
events.summary: One of the first steps taken by Slovenia's new right-wing government in June was to remove the rainbow flag outside the Culture Ministry. As Pride Month draws to a close, DW surveys the situation of the LGBTQ+ community.

events.title: Poland orders Swedish submarines to bulk up navy
events.summary: Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines in an effort to step up defense capabilities. The NATO member is modernizing its navy in the face of Russian aggression at its borders.

events.title: Germany news: Temperatures plunge after historic heat wave
events.summary: A record-breaking heat wave has moved on, with temperatures sinking rapidly amid many storms. German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul is heading to Washington for talks with his US counterpart, with NATO issues in focus.

events.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
events.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

events.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
events.summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

events.title: Germany's development aid strategy under fire
events.summary: Days ahead of the launch of the North-South Commission, development aid organizations have urged the German government to reverse billions in cuts to development aid.

events.title: Germany news: New heat record at 41.7 degrees Celsius
events.summary: For the third consecutive day, the DWD German Weather Service has reported a record temperature. The worst of the heat is moving east and it should start cooling soon.

events.title: Fact check: As Europe bakes, should you trust your sunscreen?
events.summary: As a heat wave sweeps across Europe, claims are resurfacing online questioning whether sunscreen is safe or linked to skin cancer risk.

events.title: Hungary: Magyar's 'Operation Purgatory' tackles Orban system
events.summary: The new Hungarian government is moving swiftly to dismantle the system of former Prime Minister Viktor Orban. The focus: fighting corruption, reforming the media and protecting democracy.

events.title: Albania's 'Flamingo Revolution': What's behind the protests?
events.summary: For weeks now, images of crowds of Albanians protesting on the streets have been relayed around the world. The protesters say they are fighting for democracy. PM Edi Rama insists the movement is part of a hybrid war.

events.title: Why is Germany taking a big stake in a major tankmaker?
events.summary: The German government will buy a 40% stake in tankmaker KNDS. The deal is as much about control in the critical defense sector as it is about the Franco-German relationship.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Why Germany's Deutsche Bahn will face delays for many years
events.summary: Delayed, unreliable and forced into long detours due to construction: Germany no longer sets the pace when it comes to rail travel — and this is unlikely to change anytime soon. The company is mired in a deep crisis.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: World Cup: Morocco's Ismael Saibari signs for Bayern Munich
events.summary: Three nationalities, five languages and soon to be resident in a fourth country: Ismael Saibari, the star of Morocco's World Cup so far, has signed for Bayern Munich. But he has business to finish with the Atlas Lions.

events.title: German lawyers: Ban on far-right AfD 'likely successful'
events.summary: A new report by legal experts has found that efforts to ban Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) could be successful — but there would be political dangers to such a move.

events.title: World Cup: 'Pride Match' sparks objections in Egypt and Iran
events.summary: Friday night's game in Seattle was designated as a "Pride Match" well ahead of the World Cup draw. The fixture list then threw up Egypt vs. Iran, two countries in which homosexuality is criminalized.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Magdeburg Christmas market attacker sentenced to life in prison
events.summary: A Magdeburg court has sentenced a 51-year-old to life over his 2024 attack on a Christmas market in the city. The trained psychiatrist drove an SUV through a barricade and into a crowd, killing six and injuring hundreds.

events.title: Germany disappoints in loss to Ecuador at 2026 World Cup
events.summary: Germany had already qualified for the knockouts of the 2026 World Cup, but their performance was still disappointing in a 2-1 defeat.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
events.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

events.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
events.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

events.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
events.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

events.title: World Cup: Why Mexico may be LA's favorite team
events.summary: Mexican-American football fans in the USA often split allegiances between their two cultures. For many in Los Angeles, the Mexico national team trumps the nation they call home.

events.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
events.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

events.title: Curacao's trailblazing doctor at the World Cup
events.summary: Suzanne Huurman is the only female chief doctor at the 2026 World Cup. Her work is inspiring change in men's football.

events.title: Experts axed by Trump keep climate information flowing
events.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

events.title: Schweinsteiger's Ivory Coast comments spark racism debate
events.summary: Bastian Schweinsteiger's TV analysis of Germany's World Cup opponents Ivory Coast appeared to draw on racist stereotypes. For some observers, the comments are evidence that racist tropes are still common in football.

events.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
events.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

events.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
events.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

events.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
events.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

events.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
events.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

events.title: 75 years of Germany's Goethe-Institut
events.summary: For 75 years, the Goethe-Institut has stood for cultural exchange, education and partnerships around the globe — even under challenging political conditions.

events.title: The man who knew, but didn't say: Alan Greenspan (1926-2026)
events.summary: Alan Greenspan’s reputation as head of the US Federal Reserve was legendary — not least because of his reputation for convoluted statements. However, the 2008/9 financial crisis took a toll on his standing.

events.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
events.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

events.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
events.summary: With three goals in two games, striker Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany at the World Cup. But things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.

events.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
events.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

events.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
events.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

events.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
events.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

events.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
events.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

events.title: How cities are adapting to a warming world
events.summary: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

events.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
events.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

events.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

events.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge
events.summary: His art challenges our perception: Forms vanish and perspectives shift. Anish Kapoor leads the viewer to the edge of the abyss — and sometimes, even beyond. Two new exhibitions revisit his work.

events.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
events.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

events.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
events.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

events.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
events.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

events.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
events.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

events.title: CRISPR gene-editing for crops: Precision tool or new risk?
events.summary: The EU has eased restrictions on crops developed using novel genetic techniques. While supporters say the technology could help farmers adapt to climate change, critics argue its effects remain poorly understood.

events.title: Ebola and the US patient spotlight global health 'injustice'
events.summary: The story of the US doctor who survived Ebola with experimental treatment in Germany highlights wider inequities in global health. It also reveals how sharply medical standards can differ between African countries.

events.title: Leipzig Bach festival: Encouraging dialog
events.summary: Talking, debating and listening are skills we can all learn, particularly in the light of current global conflicts. The Leipzig Bachfest shows how Johann Sebastian Bach's music can serve as a model here.

events.title: Artist JR's wrapped bridge opens in Paris in nod to Christo
events.summary: After a weather-delayed start, the French street artist's cavernous optical illusion takes over one of the most iconic bridges in Paris. It's a tribute to Christo and Jeanne-Claude's famous "Pont Neuf Wrapped."

events.title: Strait of Hormuz reopening won't end shipping risks
events.summary: A proposed US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is raising hopes for global shipping and oil markets. But naval mines, high insurance costs and geopolitical risks mean disruption could persist for months.

events.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?
events.summary: A potential US-Iran deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz could ease the global energy crunch, but oil prices and supplies may take months to stabilize as shipping restarts and infrastructure recovers.

events.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
events.summary: There are still mines in the Straight of Hormuz, delaying the opening for commercial shipping. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?

events.title: Swiss to vote on whether to cap population at 10 million
events.summary: On June 14, Swiss voters will be able to decide on a far-right initiative to curb future immigration. What will it mean for the economy — and the country's European neighbors — if they approve the proposal?

events.title: Elon Musk becomes world's first trillionaire after SpaceX IPO
events.summary: SpaceX floated on the Nasdaq stock exchange on Friday, with shares trading 25% above the opening price at one point. The IPO has made Musk the world's first trillionaire, at least on paper.

events.title: British artist David Hockney dies at 88
events.summary: Considered a superstar of pop art, the UK painter was one of the world's most influential artists during his lifetime.

events.title: Lab-grown Tyrannosaurus leather bag fails to sell at auction
events.summary: The handbag promoted as the "world's first T. rex leather product" didn't find a buyer at auction. Is it more chicken than dinosaur?

events.title: Preaching and teaching in a warming world
events.summary: US pastors, a Ugandan musician and salmon-friendly tech delivered good environmental news this week.

events.title: Is there anything GLP-1 agonist drugs cannot do?
events.summary: Every other month, researchers find new uses for GLP-1 agonist drugs, like Mounjaro and Ozempic — now even as a treatment for substance abuse. It's classic drug repurposing, a technique that saved lives during COVID.

events.title: Musk's SpaceX IPO holds both promise and peril
events.summary: SpaceX, Elon Musk's rocket and AI company, presents potential for investors. But its futuristic, unproven plans also carry significant financial risks.

events.title: Sex doll art sparks debate at German president's residence
events.summary: Beyond a headline-grabbing bronze, contemporary artworks draw attention to political power at an exhibition held at Bellevue Palace before the state building closes for renovation.

events.title: 'Disclosure Day': For Spielberg, aliens are not just fiction
events.summary: As Steven Spielberg releases a new film on extraterrestrials, here's a brief history of modern alien conspiracies and why scientists are now taking UFO reports seriously.

events.title: The climate conversation many US pastors avoid
events.summary: As the Trump administration undoes scores of environmental protections, some Christian leaders are inviting their congregations to do as the Bible asks and be good stewards of the land. But many others stay silent.

events.title: Leipzig Bachfest 2026: Bach's music as hit parade
events.summary: The Leipzig Bach Festival honors the composer with a hit parade. Over 8,000 fans from 20 countries cast their votes. But Bach is celebrated in many other countries around the world as well.

events.title: Why Anthropic has the edge over OpenAI in IPO race
events.summary: The rivalry between Sam Altman and Dario Amodei is entering a new phase. Both AI bosses are planning public listings and hope to use the money to outpace each other in the race to build superintelligent AI.

events.title: Depression in romantic relationships: You, me and the illness
events.summary: Depression can be a huge challenge for a relationship. Loved ones of someone with depression often feel helpless. But there is actually a lot they can do ― and a lot they shouldn't.

events.title: Jafar Panahi's prison sentence confirmed by Iranian court
events.summary: Following a retrial for a verdict that had been reached while the prominent Iranian director was abroad, the Revolutionary Court in Tehran has upheld a one-year jail sentence.

events.title: Algeria's ruling FLN secures most seats in parliament with historically low turnout
events.summary: Algeria's ruling National Liberation Front won the most seats in the country's parliamentary elections, the election board said Monday, securing 90 out of 407 in the July 2 vote. But the poll was marred by record-low turnout after the government barred more than a third of prospective candidates from running.

events.title: France at 'turning point' on how to prevent and deal with sexual violence
events.summary: In a new twist in a femicide case that has gripped France for years, Cédric Jubillar has admitted to the murder of his wife and has told authorities he would help them find her body. Delphine Jubillar had disappeared in late December 2020 as she was asking for divorce and had started a new relationship. FRANCE 24 Carys Garland tells us more about the case, and the growing tension in France over the killing of women and children.

events.title: 'His resilience, his words, his character': Mourners grieve late supreme leader Khamenei
events.summary: A sea of mourners filled the streets of Tehran on Monday for the funeral procession of late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. FRANCE 24 spoke to some of those who had travelled across the country to pay their respects for the man who led the Islamic Republic for almost four decades until he was killed in US-Israeli air strikes.

events.title: Avignon turns 80: Wagner Moura, Isabelle Huppert attend France's oldest theatre festival
events.summary: FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear brings us all the latest from the Avignon performing arts festival, which is celebrating its 80th anniversary. This year, Golden Globe winners, Brazilian actor Wagner Moura and French actress Isabelle Huppert are starring in shows. The festival has also chosen Korean as its language of honour and turns the spotlight on artists from the Mediterranean region. Salazar-Winspear also talks to the co-director of Avignon's fringe program. It's known as the "off" in French and features more than 1,700 shows performed in public spaces around the city. Finally, she gives us her recommendation for a fringe show that is not to be missed.

events.title: Earthquake damage threatens Venezuela's beach tourism centers
events.summary: The earthquakes that hit Venezuela in late June have devastated seaside towns where tourism has been a vital economic lifeline for an already struggling population. Also, tanker traffic picks up through the Strait of Hormuz, and Europe seeks to convince the US that it's boosting defence spending.

events.title: Does the French right have a chance of defeating the far right in the election?
events.summary: Right-wing Edouard Philippe has officially launched his presidential campaign less than a year ahead of the 2027 election in France. Philippe was the first one to say explicitly he would run for France's highest office. Now, polls give the ex-PM the best chance to defeat the far right. FRANCE 24's Marc Perelman tells us more.

events.title: Iranians struggle as ongoing economic crisis shows no sign of abatting
events.summary: Across Iran, families are grappling with soaring living costs, as the combined weight of war, subsidy reforms and sanctions pushes basic necessities beyond the reach of many. Some Iranians had hoped foreign intervention would unseat the regime - instead, the US-Israel war has crushed livelihoods.

events.title: In pictures: Marine Le Pen’s political rise and frustrated presidential ambitions
events.summary: French far-right figurehead Marine Le Pen could see her 40-year political career come to an end on Tuesday if French courts rule that she is guilty of embezzling public funds and ineligible to stand for the presidency in 2027. FRANCE 24 takes a look at how she rose to prominence in France and brought her National Rally party into mainstream politics.

events.title: How are the millions attending Khamenei's funeral remembering the late ayatollah?
events.summary: The funeral procession for Iran's late supreme leader Ali Khamenei has begun in Tehran. Millions are expected to attend, possibly leading to crowds that could rival those that turned out for his predecessor nearly four decades ago. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah went to the procession and tells us why so many admired the late ayatollah.

events.title: Greece's Thessaloniki threatened by spreading wildfire
events.summary: A fast-moving wildfire broke out in Greece's second largest city on July 4, forcing residents of three small suburbs north of the city to evacuate. The fire ignited a ravine in a mountainous area and spread quickly, with strong winds hampering efforts to contain the fire. According to the country's public broadcaster, a 76-year-old man was arrested on suspicion of starting the wildfire, as he allegedly ignited the blaze after his vehicle produced sparks.

events.title: Dissolution of Hamas's Gaza governing body won't bring 'significant change of life'
events.summary: Hamas announced on July 6 the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades. The decision would clear the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule. But questions remain. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more from Jerusalem.

events.title: World Cup 2026: England hold on against Mexico while Norway oust Brazil
events.summary: The Three Lions defied high altitude and a roaring home crowd to beat Mexico 3-2. Man of the match Jude Bellingham scored twice in the space of two minutes while Harry Kane's penalty kept England ahead of a fierce Mexico team. Meanwhile five-time champions Brazil were knocked-out by Norway, as Erling Haaland did what he does best to seal the win 2-1.

events.title: As a new heatwave looms, Europe falls for Chinese air conditioners
events.summary: Hundreds of people besieged supermarkets in and around Paris, with scuffles and shouting matches breaking out as residents scrambled to get their hands on bargain air-cooling units before the next heatwave. With the hunt for affordability, Europeans are turning to Chinese AC units - despite trade tensions between Beijing and Brussels. FRANCE 24's Yena Lee tells us more.

events.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
'Genuine admiration and emotion' in Tehran's streets as Iran mourns supreme leader
events.summary: Vast crowds have gathered for the funeral procession of Iran's slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei in Tehran on July 6, with authorities estimating millions were on the streets. The final public tribute is expected to last around 10 hours. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah tells us more on what to know.

events.title: Venezuelans mobilise to deal with earthquakes' consequences: FRANCE 24 reports
events.summary: As more than ten days have passed since Venezuela was struck by twin earthquakes, the death toll remains uncertain, with an official balance of at least 3,342 people killed. On the northern coastal part of the country, the hardest-hit region, survivors and volunteers from across the country are taking matters into their own hands : treating the injured, finding shelter, searching for the missing. FRANCE 24's Pascale Mariani and Maxime Pluvinet spoke to residents

events.title: Hamas dissolves Gaza governing body, clearing way for technocratic committee
events.summary: The Palestinian movement Hamas announced Monday that it had dissolved its governing body in the Gaza Strip after nearly 20 years in power, paving the way for a technocratic committee to administer the territory.

events.title: Moldova speeds up its membership process to the EU
events.summary: An economically fragile country caught up in Russia’s hybrid war, Moldova is keen to make rapid progress for a quicker membership to the European Union. With the opening of the first chapter of accession negotiations, the EU launched its  €1.9 billion investment plan in the country. With this plan, which is conditional on domestic structural reforms,  Europe hopes to draw in businesses and drive change in the country.

events.title: Macron's visit to Syria confirms normalisation strategy of post-Assad government
events.summary: Syria is expecting its first Western European head of state since new leader Ahmed al-Sharaa swept to power in 2024. A Syrian state news agency reported that France's Emmanuel Macron will visit to discuss strengthening bilateral relations. It wouldn't be the first time the two have met. In May 2025, Macron welcomed al-Sharaa to the Élysée Palace, where they discussed issues of security and Macron addressed the interreligious violence in Syria.

events.title: At least 14 killed in Kyiv after second massive Russian attack
events.summary: Kyiv has been struck by Russian missiles for the second time in less than a week, with at least 14 people killed. While Russia says it hit military-industrial targets and energy sites, the morning strikes hit residential areas in the early hours of the morning. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg tells us more from Odesa.

events.title: 'Like we won a final': England savour dramatic win and other World Cup news
events.summary: England has won a nerve-racking World Cup classic 3-2 against Mexico on July 6 to reach the quarterfinals, after Jude Bellingham scored two goals and Harry Kanes scored a penalty. Norway has also made history by reaching the round of 8 for the first time, after disqualifying Brazil. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us more on what to know from the latest on the World Cup.

events.title: Supporters stopped from attending Tour de France as wildfires ravage South of France
events.summary: Excess heat and wildfires are affecting the Tour de France cycle race. The third stage of the race on July 6 will go near an area where wildfires have broken out in France's southwest. Local authorities and race organisers as a result have asked people not to go to the race route to cheer riders on.

events.title: Syria expecting Macron visit in first for Western head-of-state since Assad
events.summary: Syria is expecting a visit by French President Emmanuel Macron, the first by a Western European head of state since Syria's new leader Ahmed al-Sharaa took power in 2024. Macron would be accompanied by a delegation "including investors and representatives of French companies". FRANCE 24's Matthew-Mary Caruchet tells us what we know.

events.title: At least 25 people killed after Sri Lankan prison riot
events.summary: At least 25 people have been killed and more than 100 injured after a huge riot between inmates at a Sri Lankan prison.

events.title: 'Restraining order needed': Trump escalates Meloni feud
events.summary: Donald Trump has escalated his feud with Giorgia Meloni with a new goading social media post.

events.title: Russian spy plane dropped sonar trackers in path of Royal Navy aircraft carrier
events.summary: A Russian spy plane dropped tens of sonar devices in the path of the UK's flagship aircraft carrier in a suspected attempt to harass the warship that prompted RAF fighter jets to scramble.

events.title: China carries out ballistic missile test hours after warning neighbouring countries
events.summary: China carried out a long-range ballistic missile test in the south Pacific on Monday, sparking criticism from New Zealand and Australia.

events.title: Thousands flee homes as wildfires rage in southern Europe
events.summary: Thousands of people have been evacuated from their homes as wildfires burn through large areas of southern Europe, including in popular holiday destinations.

events.title: Son of woman murdered in Crete 17 years ago says fight 'not over' as killer still free
events.summary: The son of a Scottish woman who was murdered in Crete 17 years ago has vowed the fight for justice is "not over" as the killer remains free pending an appeal.

events.title: Chinese pastor freed after Donald Trump raised issue of his release with Xi Jinping
events.summary: A Chinese pastor has been released from jail after Donald Trump intervened to get him freed, in what his family have described as a "miracle".

events.summary: A days-long funeral is under way in Tehran for Iran's former supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

events.summary: Britain has imposed sanctions on nine Russian people and entities for developing chemical weapons

events.summary: Nigel Farage is facing mounting scrutiny over undeclared financial gifts including a 5 million pound donation from a Thailand-based cryptocurrency billionaire

events.summary: Tanzanian authorities have arrested dozens of people ahead of planned anti-government protests

events.title: Sky agrees to buy British broadcaster ITV for up $2.1B to compete with streaming
events.summary: Sky, the UK-based TV, internet and mobile phone provider owned by Comcast, has agreed to buy ITV’s media and entertainment arm for up to 1.6 billion pounds, which is $2.1 billion

events.title: More than 300 children killed in Sudan war in 6 months, UNICEF says
events.summary: The U.N. children's agency reports over 300 children have been killed or injured in Sudan in the first six months of the year, mostly from drone strikes

events.title: Gold rings around 2,000 years old found during dig at Thailand archaeological site
events.summary: Officials in Thailand have discovered two gold rings that are around 2,000 years old

events.summary: Australia and Fiji have signed a new bilateral defense alliance in a second major diplomatic win within a year for Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese against Chinese influence in the South Pacific

events.summary: Over 1,000 schools across the U.K. have been forced to close during Europe's recent record-smashing heat wave

events.title: Canada to decide between German, South Korean submarine bids on Monday
events.summary: Canada is expected Monday to choose between German and South Korean bids to build a multibillion-dollar fleet of 12 submarines

events.summary: The Philippine Senate has started the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte in a politically volatile event that will unfold against the backdrop of her feud with President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. Thousands of police officers were deployed to s...

events.summary: Hundreds of firefighters are battling wildfires in Portugal, Spain and Greece

events.title: Pope on vacation after decisive flexing of papal muscle
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV has had a whirlwind finale to the first half of 2026.

events.summary: Nigeria reports that two of its nationals were killed in South Africa following anti-immigrant protests targeting African workers

events.title: Heavy rains leave 5 dead in China's north; tropical storm hits the south and Vietnam
events.summary: Heavy rains have left five people dead in northern China, while a tropical storm has toppled tree and submerged cars in the south, according to state media

events.title: British military says cargo ship attacked in Red Sea off coast of Yemen
events.summary: A skiff approached the ship and opened fire, then sailed back to a larger ship.

events.title: 1 killed in attack on Crimea as Putin and Zelenskyy hold separate Trump calls

events.title: Inside Tehran as funeral for late Iranian supreme leader
events.summary: NBC News’ Richard Engel is in Tehran where huge crowds are gathering for the funeral of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Our team was at the mosque where mourners were chanting “death to America.” The ayatollah was killed in American and Israeli airstrikes at the start of the war earlier this year.

events.title: Iran says millions expected at funeral for former supreme leader
events.summary: Foreign dignitaries and senior Iranian leaders honored Ayatollah Ali Khamenei today, months after he was killed by the United States and Israel. Iran says organizers expect 15 to 20 million people at his dayslong state funeral. NBC News' Richard Engel reports from Tehran.

events.title: Neil the Seal is once again wreaking havoc in the streets of Tasmania
events.summary: Neil the Seal, Tasmania's most famous Elephant Seal, makes his much-anticipated return to the Australian island after nearly six months at sea.

events.title: World Cup player from Cape Verde accused of rape
events.summary: A woman, according to her attorney, is accusing the captain of Cape Verde's National Soccer Team, Ryan Mendes, of rape. Jesse Kirsch spoke with the alleged victim's attorney.

events.title: Prince Harry’s U.K. trip sparks fresh dispute over Buckingham Palace stay
events.summary: Prince Harry is heading back to the U.K., but he won’t stay at Buckingham Palace. A new dispute over his accommodation is sparking further speculation about the acrimonious relationship between King Charles III and his youngest son.

events.title: China conducts rare long-range missile test, rattling U.S. allies
events.summary: The Chinese military test-fired a missile from a nuclear submarine into the Pacific Ocean on Monday, state media reported, drawing criticism from U.S. allies in the region concerned about Beijing’s intensifying military activity.

events.title: Chinese pastor released from prison after Trump raised his case with Xi Jinping
events.summary: Ezra Jin Mingri, who founded Beijing’s Zion Church, was detained in October in one of China’s largest crackdowns on a single church in decades.

events.title: Millions expected at delayed Khamenei funeral as Iran seeks to project strength
events.summary: Iran says millions are expected to turn out for the funeral, which will serve as a grand religious and political spectacle months after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed.

events.title: Rare copy of the Declaration of Independence seized by the British in 1776 is found
events.summary: A rare copy of the Declaration of Independence lost for 250 years has been discovered in London, where it is the only known example of its kind outside the U.S.

events.title: England fans can drink until 5 a.m. for Mexico game
events.summary: After public outcry, outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer staged a last-minute intervention to let pubs stay open, but police have raised concerns.

events.title: Iran and Oman propose fee plan for Strait of Hormuz, sources say
events.summary: Monetizing the strait would be a dramatic change from before the war, when cargo ships transited without paying any fee.

events.title: South Africa says over 900 arrested during nationwide anti-migrant protests
events.summary: Thousands have fled South Africa, fearing they will be attacked or killed, after an anti-immigrant movement set a “deadline” for them to leave the country.

events.title: Ocean surface temperatures hit record high, fanning scientists’ concerns of global crisis
events.summary: “The planet is warming because we’re emitting vast quantities of greenhouse gases, primarily from fossil fuel burning,” one expert said.

events.title: Video of a newborn and his mother rescued after days under rubble offers hope in Venezuela
events.summary: An initial estimate of 50,000 unaccounted for is “terrifyingly plausible,” according to the United Nations.

events.title: Russian satellite communications center hit, Ukraine says, as drones target Moscow
events.summary: The governor of the Moscow region said a 6-month-old died and three people were injured in the “large-scale attack.”

events.title: Some Venezuelans deported from the U.S. before quakes are missing or dead, families say
events.summary: A young man who was killed had planned a reunion with his mother, and a man who’s missing had just called a relative minutes earlier.

events.title: 3-year-old shot dead in Gaza as U.N. report accuses Israel of targeting children
events.summary: The U.N. report alleged Israel has a “deliberate strategy” of targeting children in the occupied West Bank and Gaza Strip, which Israel rejects.

events.title: Wife and children of Argentine soccer player Lucas Trejo killed in Venezuela earthquakes
events.summary: Trejo described his desperate attempts to dig through the rubble to find his family; the wife of another soccer player, Hector Bello, died shielding their baby when the earthquake struck.

events.title: Time is running out to rescue those trapped in Venezuela, officials say
events.summary: The death toll has risen to 1,719 amid growing criticism of the government’s response.

events.title: Andy Burnham says he’ll transform the U.K. economy by giving local leaders greater autonomy
events.summary: The former mayor of Greater Manchester is the strong favorite to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who announced his resignation last week.
